architecture_notes: A100 SXM4 (Ampere): 108 SMs. Ridge point ~250 FLOP/byte. warp_cycles_per_instruction counter unavailable on this profile (null for all operators); eligible_cycles_pct used as the latency-bound proxy instead. Both dominant kernels (ampere_sgemm_128x32_tn and ampere_sgemm_128x128_nn) are cuBLAS FP32 SIMT paths — confirmed by smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active = 0 on the dominant kernel. Dedup profiling pattern detected: layer::unique::modules_0 was the profiled representative; modules_1 through modules_7 received propagated metrics. The 448 individual aten::mm / aten::bmm entries with kernel_count=1 represent per-operation dedup views below the 1% threshold; their aggregate is captured in aten::mm_0 and aten::bmm_0.
optimization_ceiling: Top 2 operators account for 55.8% of attributed time. Both are tensor_core_idle: casting inputs from FP32 to BF16 or FP16 would route all GEMM and BMM calls to the Tensor Core pipeline (HMMA), expected to yield 4-8x throughput improvement on these kernels and recover the dominant fraction of total runtime. aten::bmm_0 additionally shows severe wave starvation (waves_per_sm=0.296 on the dominant kernel, achieved_occupancy=12.5%) — QKV fusion to increase the effective matrix size, or larger batch size, would improve Tensor Core utilization further once the dtype is corrected.
